question: What matters most in a browser?
subtitle: Pick the one thing you can't compromise on

options.label: Speed & Performance
options.description: I want everything to load instantly

options.label: Privacy & Security
options.description: My data is not for sale

options.label: Features & Tools
options.description: Give me everything built-in

options.label: Simplicity
options.description: Just get out of my way

question: How do you mainly use your browser?
subtitle: Pick the mode that describes most of your time online

options.label: Work & Productivity
options.description: Docs, meetings, research, work apps

options.label: Gaming & Entertainment
options.description: Streaming, social media, YouTube, gaming news

options.label: Privacy & Research
options.description: Sensitive topics, journalism, staying under the radar

options.label: Casual Everyday
options.description: Shopping, news, email, quick searches

question: How do you feel about customization?
subtitle: Be honest — do you actually change your settings?

options.label: Don't touch my settings
options.description: Defaults are fine, I just want it to work

options.label: Some tweaks
options.description: A few extensions and a nice theme

options.label: I want control over everything
options.description: Custom CSS, flags, about:config — all of it

question: What's your relationship with Big Tech?
subtitle: How much do you rely on — or avoid — major tech platforms?

options.label: All in
options.description: Google, Apple, or Microsoft — I'm deep in one ecosystem and I like it

options.label: Convenient, not committed
options.description: I use their services but I'm not loyal to any one company

options.label: Skeptical
options.description: I use them when I have to, but I'd rather not

options.label: Independent
options.description: I actively avoid Big Tech wherever possible

question: What's your stance on ads and trackers?
subtitle: Choose the option that feels most like you

options.label: I don't mind them
options.description: Ads keep the internet free — fair enough

options.label: Block ads, but I'm flexible
options.description: An extension is enough, I don't overthink it

options.label: Block everything by default
options.description: Built-in protection is a must — no exceptions

options.label: Maximum anonymity
options.description: Anti-fingerprinting and Tor-level protection, please

question: Do you care if your browser is open source?
subtitle: Transparency and community governance — does it matter to you?

options.label: Not really
options.description: I just want it to work well

options.label: Nice to have
options.description: I appreciate transparency, but it's not a dealbreaker

options.label: It's a must
options.description: I only trust software I — or others — can verify

question: What's your ideal browser's look and feel?
subtitle: The vibe question — trust your gut

options.label: Clean and familiar
options.description: Like Chrome, but maybe a little better

options.label: Minimal and zen
options.description: Beautiful, quiet, no clutter — just the web

options.label: Feature-packed dashboard
options.description: Sidebar, workspaces, panels, tools — give it all to me

options.label: Unique and experimental
options.description: I want something no other browser does
